summary: Checked the iteration-3 Define-phase artifacts (requirements.md R1-R34, open_questions.md, discovery_notes.md) against guides/backend-guide.md and guides/ui-style-guide.md. The work under review is deliberately non-technical business requirements plus discovery/open-questions notes; there is no code, design, or diff, so the code-structural rules (services with .call -> Result, thin controllers, no business logic in callbacks/jobs, Tailwind type/spacing scale, semantic status colors, smallest-DOM-unit Turbo targeting, Minitest) have nothing concrete to bind against yet and correctly defer to design/build, where this critic re-engages. Where the specs touch guide-governed surfaces they align rather than deviate: R4 (paused/held state) and R26 (menu re-run failure) both require the state be shown 'using a label and not color alone,' honoring the UI guide accessibility baseline (status never conveyed by color alone); R31 mandates plain, non-technical language, consistent with the Define-stays-non-technical constraint; the new R12 (single-step re-run in-flight indicator) mirrors R16's 'restart in progress' treatment and the existing step-card live-progress/aria-live precedent recorded in the discovery notes, which is consistent with the UI guide's live-region and 'smallest DOM unit' broadcast rules. The iteration-3 revisions (adding R12, removing the ambiguous 'resume' language, renumbering) introduce no new guide-governed prescription and no deviation. The discovery notes accurately restate the mandatory guide constraints and map every proposed capability onto existing service POROs (Phases::SendBack target_step_id, AnswerQuestions, Approve, StepRuns::Queue, StepRuns::BroadcastCard) rather than callbacks, fat controllers, or model business logic. No requirement prescribes an approach that would deviate from either guide. Non-blocking observation carried from iteration 2: R12/R16's in-progress states do not restate the label-not-color-alone rule the way R4/R26 do, but they describe a live progress indicator rather than a status color, and the principle is established doc-wide and carries into design. No blockers or deviations found.
